text.val: There are numerous organizations offering training in different topics such as gender planning, gender-sensitive approaches to resource allocation, community engagement, gender impact assessment, gender-responsive climate finance and others. These and related courses are offered by several UN agencies such as UN Women and ITC ILO, through online and blended training platforms.

title.val: Gender equality and social inclusion training

text.val: Allocate financial and technical resources to organizational and intervention budgets to meet gender and inclusion integration needs. The use of gender-responsive budgeting (GRB) tools can assist in facilitating this process.

Institutions and organisations involved in drought management should orient staff on gender analytical frameworks and tools that are commonly used to generate information and data on gender and social inclusion aspects. This vital information and data should subsequently be incorporated in the drought planning and management process.

title.val: Embed gender and social equity in planning

text.val: Gender integration should become routine professional practice, with gender considerations included at all stages of drought planning—assessment, implementation, and monitoring. Gender-specific vulnerabilities and needs must receive equal attention. Monitoring mechanisms and gender-disaggregated data are essential to evaluate impacts, assess effectiveness, and identify improvements. Accountability mechanisms are crucial for tracking GESI in organizational practices and programs.

Strengthen collaboration and knowledge sharing among staff with different expertise including gender specialists, climate experts and community development practitioners. Facilitate knowledge sharing on best practices for gender-inclusive drought planning and management.

ops.insert: Shared commitment to gender equality by organizations

Promoting a shared commitment to gender equality in drought planning and management requires an organization to equip its staff with the understanding, skills and support necessary to integrate gender considerations into all aspects of planning and management.
Check some of the key strategies that can be employed to achieve this shared commitment!
